page: Vultr vbm-6c-128gb is a bare metal server configured with an Intel Xeon 2386G processor. This x86_64 architecture CPU features 6 physical cores and 12 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory allocation of 10.67 GB per core.
For local storage, the server includes a bundled 1920 GB SSD. It does not feature any dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Given the high memory-to-core ratio and dedicated bare metal resources, this configuration is designed for memory-intensive workloads, database hosting, and applications requiring local SSD storage. The lack of virtualization overhead on bare metal hardware provides consistent performance for resource-dense environments.

With its high memory-to-CPU ratio and dedicated bare metal architecture, this instance is suited for memory-optimized workloads, database deployments, and applications requiring consistent local storage performance without virtualization overhead.
